title: Create the Gapminder dataset
text:
# Import necessary libraries
import pandas as pd
from gapminder import gapminder

# Rename 'lifeExp' to 'life_expectancy' and 'gdpPercap' to 'gdp_per_capita'
gapminder = gapminder.rename(columns={'lifeExp': 'life_expectancy',
                                      'pop': 'population_millions', 
                                      'gdpPercap': 'gdp_per_capita'})

# Convert population to millions
gapminder['population_millions'] = gapminder['population_millions'] / 1_000_000

# Create a new pandas DataFrame from the modified gapminder data
gapminder_df = pd.DataFrame(gapminder)

# Save the DataFrame as a CSV file
gapminder_df.to_csv('gapminder.csv', index=False)

# Print the first 3 rows of the DataFrame
print(gapminder_df.head(3))

title: Life Expectancy and GDP per Capita
text:
This page analyzes the relationship between life expectancy and GDP per capita over time using the Gapminder dataset.

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

plt.figure()
plt.scatter(df["gdp_per_capita"], df["life_expectancy"])
plt.xlabel("GDP per Capita")
plt.ylabel("Life Expectancy")
plt.title("Life Expectancy vs GDP per Capita")
plt.show()

title: Country Analysis
text: This page analyzes changes in life expectancy, GDP per capita, and population over time for India using the Gapminder dataset.

title: Country Analysis
text: This page analyzes changes in life expectancy, GDP per capita, and population over time for India using the Gapminder dataset.

title: Country Analysis
text:
India Over Time

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")
country_df = df[df["country"] == "India"]

fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.plot(country_df["year"], country_df["life_expectancy"])
ax.set_xlabel("Year")
ax.set_ylabel("Life Expectancy")
ax.set_title("India Life Expectancy Over Time")

fig

title: Country Analysis
text:
Interactive Map (Bonus)

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.io as pio

# Ensure plotly renders correctly in Quarto
pio.renderers.default = "notebook"

df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")
df_2007 = df[df["year"] == 2007]

fig = px.choropleth(
    df_2007,
    locations="country",
    locationmode="country names",
    color="life_expectancy",
    hover_name="country",
    color_continuous_scale="Viridis",
    title="Life Expectancy by Country (2007)"
)

fig.update_layout(margin=dict(l=0, r=0, t=50, b=0))

fig

title: Gapminder Dataset Website
text: The Gapminder dataset contains information on life expectancy, GDP per capita, and population for 142 countries from 1952 to 2007 at 5-year intervals.

title: Test Page
text:
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.scatter(df["population_millions"], df["life_expectancy"])
ax.set_xlabel("Population (Millions)")
ax.set_ylabel("Life Expectancy")
ax.set_title("Life Expectancy vs Population")

fig
